ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, APPLICATION FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANTS KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC., AND K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. IS GRANTED. THE COURT FINDS FOR PLAINTIFF AND SHALL RECOVER DAMAGES FROM DEFENDANTS KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC., AND K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. IN THE AMOUNTS AS FOLLOWS: AS TO KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC.: ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $3,120.45; NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $2,000,000.00; TOTAL OF ECONOMIC DAMAGES AND NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $2,003,120.45; AND AS TO K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC.: ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $3,120.45; NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $2,000,000.00; TOTAL OF ECONOMIC DAMAGES AND NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES: $2,003,120.45. JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY. REPORTER: FTR REPORTED (304/EPS). THE JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF FISAGA, TAUALAI RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT KELLY-MOORE PAINT COMPANY, INC. K-M INDUSTRIES HOLDING CO., INC. $2,003,120.45 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,003,120.45, SEE SCANNED DOC

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar Nov-08-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Sep-10-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a).

1. The trial date is December 30, 2024, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
a. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is January 8, 2025.
b. The parties shall follow the California Rules of Court, San Francisco Local Rules, and Local Rule 20.

2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 24, 2024.
a. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise.
b. Before a party files and serves a summary judgment/adjudication motion, it must contact the clerk to make a reservation.
c. The Court allows a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared, unless good cause is found to exceed this number. Contact the clerk to schedule a good cause hearing.

3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
a. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier.
b. Any issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, shall occur in person or via telephone, not by email or letter.

4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is December 13, 2024.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is December 27, 2024.

7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than October 10, 2024.
a. If Plaintiff submits documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify Defendants no later than five days after submission.
b. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.

8. No later than September 24, 2024:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion. (Mini-minutes continue in Part 2 of 2.)

PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF (ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR TENTATIVE RULNG ENTRY PURPOSES ONLY)
Off Calendar Sep-10-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
(Part 2 of 2.)
(Tentative ruling continued from previous entry.)

9. A further case management conference is set for November 12, 2024 at 1:30 p.m.
a. Case management conference statements are not required.
b. Parties may appear at the conference by zoom.
c. If the parties agree that no CMC is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two court days before the hearing.

10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than November 18, 2024. Response to the settlement demand is due December 2, 2024. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on December 9, 2024.

11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, December 26, 2024, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Matter Not Reported. Court Reporter: Bailiff: Not Present. (301/RCE)
